Admission Date:  1996-10-20              Discharge Date:   2005-11-20


Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Best
Chief Complaint:
malaise.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none


History of Present Illness:
Pt is a [**Age over 90 **] yo male with h/o Meneire's, syncope s/p pacemaker,
BPH, who presented to ED on 11-07 with malaise and worsening
shaking movements and found to have an NSTEMI. Pt says that the
day prior he woke up "shaking" and with dizziness. It was not
true vertigo but pt felt as though "my head was spinning." It
was difficult for him to get out of bed. +cough with some yellow
sputum and nasal congestion. No SOB when walks, though pt was
unable to walk 07-10 above. Normally, he is able to walk blocks
without a problem. Madeline Rodriguez orthopnea/PND. No chest pain. No
F/C/muscle aches. Pt is unable to say if he has had these
shaking movements before.
.
In the ED, VS on arrival were T: 100.4; HR: 97; BP: 110/56; RR:
24; O2: 96 RA. He was given 1 L NS. He was found to have an
NSTEMI with troponin of 3.27, CK 383, MB 55. He was given 325 mg
ASA, metoprolol 5 mg IV, metoprolol 12.5 mg po, simvastatin 80
mg po. Cardiology fellow was contactMadeline Rodriguez and agreed with Madeline Rodriguez.
Heparin gtt and integrillin were started. Pt remained in the ED
overnight and was stable throught the day awaiting a bed. Pt
subsequently had a guaiac positive black stool. PTT was 54.9.
Heparin and Integrillin gtts were stopped. Pt had 3 more stools
that were melanotic. Was given a 500cc NS bolus, and 40mg IV
protonix. NG tube was placed and revealed 400cc maroon colored
blood. NG tube was left to intermittent suction. He was
transfused 1 unit pRBC's. GI was consulted and pt refused
endoscopy.
.
In the MICU, the pt's NSTEMI was treated conservatively (ASA,
BB, statin) as the pt did not want cardiac cath. He was
transfused an additional unit of PRBC's. He was also found to
have an infiltrate on CXR, and was placed on Abx. Pt's hct
remained stable after anticoagulation was stopped. He is now
being transferred to the floor.
.
Today, pt denies SOB or chest pain.


Past Medical History:
meneire's disease
h/o syncope, s/p pacemaker placement
BPH, s/p prostate surgery
lower back surgery years ago
RBBB
cataracts, s/p surgery
hard of hearing


Social History:
Social: married, lives in 4870 Kristin Stream
West Todd, VA 77276-3107
+ cigs ( one pck every 36 hours) x many years
+ wine every night


Family History:

NAD

clear;

Abd: +BS. soft, nt, nd.
Back: No spinal, paraspinal, CVA tenderness
Ext: No edema. DP 1+
Neuro: CN II-XII tested and intact. MS 04-05 throughout. Reflexes:
biceps, brachio, achilles 07-10. A&O x 3

Brief Hospital Course:
[**Age over 90 **] yo man with h/o meniere's disease and syncope s/p pacemaker
placement who presented with NSTEMI and likely UGIB in setting
of anticoagulation with heparin and integrillin. Heparin and
integrillin were stopped, and pt's hct has remained relatively
stable (pt received total 3 U PRBC's to keep hct>30).
.
# GI bleeding: likely UGIB 07-10 underlying ulcer vs. malignancy
vs. AVM vs. varices in setting of anticoagulation with heparin
and integrillin. GI service followed, however pt refussed
endoscopy.  The decisions was made to stop the pt's integrilling
and heparin, however aspirin was restarted once pt's hct
stabilized.
-pt recieved 3 U PRBC's, and bumped appropriately (Hct 33.4 on
discharge)
-goal Hct>30 was given NSTEMI
-IV protonix was given Medical Center Of Trinity, changed to PO on discharge
.
# NSTEMI: Pt had elevated trop on admission up 3.27. Pt was
started on heparin and integrillin, but pt refused cardiac
catherization.  After development of GI bleed, heparin and
integrilin were stopped. Aspirin was briefly held, but was then
restarted.
- IV metoprolol was given, and changed to PO on discharge
-atorvastatin 80mg daily
- pt should be started on an ACE inhibitor in the near future
(once he is shown to be hemodynamically stable for several days
s/p GI bleedd)
.
# ?LLL infiltrate: pt had increased cough and questionable
infiltrate on CXR.
-treated with 3 days of IV azithromycin and ceftriaxone.
Switched to PO levo on discharge for total 10 day course of Abx.

# h/o meniere's - held meclizine in house, can be restarted if
symptoms recur as an outpatient.
.
# bph - continued finasteride
.
# FEN: clear liquid diet
.
# Ppx: no heparin given bleeding. pneumoboots, on ppi.
.
# contact: son Alexis Green Angelica Brooks MD 940-519-2311. Was contactMadeline Rodriguez by Dr.
Long after pt arrived to MICU. Dr. Long expressed pt's wishes
for no scope and DNR/DNI and son was agreeable.
.
# dispo: rehab
.
# code: DNR/DNI


Medications on Admission:
Finasteride 5 mg qday
Meclizine 12.5 mg prn

1. Atorvastatin 80 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*

4. Metoprolol Tartrate 25 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO BID (2
times a day).
5. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
6. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed for constipation.
7. Protonix 40 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: One (1)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO twice a day.
8. Levofloxacin 250 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO once a day
for 7 days.
9. Outpatient Lab Work
please have your hematocrit checked this Monday. Have the
results faxed to Dr.Long.

Discharge Instructions:
Please seek medical attention immediately if you experience
chest pain, shortness of breath,fevers, chills, nausea,
vomiting, or lightheadedness.

Please take all medications as prescribed.

Please attend all follow-up appointments.

Followup Instructions:
please follow-up with your PCP, Castro. Long, in the next 04-30
weeks.  His office number is 966-356-2302.

Please have your hematocrit checked this Monday, and have
results faxed to Dr.Joshua Graves office.
